elements.description: Two duplex receptacle outlets (⊖) at +0.30M side by side near the top wall of นอน 2 (note this floor's outlets are mounted much lower than the ground floor's +1.20M -- consistent with bedroom low-level convenience outlets vs. counter-height outlets downstairs)
Two duplex receptacle outlets (⊖) at +0.30M side by side near the top wall of นอน 3
WH junction box (circled 'WH') in the upper-floor ห้องน้ำ, feeding circuit '6/LP' toward the LP panel
Duplex receptacle outlet (⊖) at +0.30M in the upper-floor ห้องน้ำ, same wall run as the WH box

'4/LP' circuit tag at the stair landing marking the feed point for the upper-floor receptacle circuit (circuit 4 of panel LP); an 'LP' text label also appears here, most likely marking the conduit riser down to the ground-floor panel
Duplex receptacle outlets (⊖) at +0.30M, two near the top wall and two near the exterior wall of นอน 1

Incoming service run at the bottom of the sheet: '2-10 IEC 01-25 mm.PVC นอกอาคารเดินลอย' exterior exposed run labeled 'TO kWH METER', shown running down through ระเบียง toward the meter position implied off-sheet
'5/+3.65/3' elevation callout for กันสาด (canopy) at the ระเบียง edge, matching the same marker seen on EE-04's equivalent balcony/canopy area

warnings: GRID: same as EE-03/EE-04/EE-05 -- a house-wide grid master (บ้าน_เล็ก_2ชั้น_01_หน้า00_gridline.json) appeared partway through this extraction batch; this page's independently-read dimension chain matches the master's named+dummy grid exactly.

Outlet mounting height on this sheet is consistently +0.30M, clearly distinct from the +1.20M height used throughout EE-05 (ground floor) -- transcribed exactly as printed, not assumed to be a labeling error; this is a genuine convention difference between the two floors' receptacle heights (bedrooms at low/skirting height vs. ground-floor kitchen/counter height).
No boxed/waterproof-cover (⊟) outlet symbol was confidently identified anywhere on this sheet, same observation as EE-05.

This sheet is markedly higher-confidence than its sibling EE-05/EE-03/EE-04 sheets because the +0.30M outlet symbols and their per-room grouping are visually cleaner/less cluttered on this floor's plan (fewer overlapping wiring lines).

This sheet also contains a telephone/TV/AC-circuit notes column (general_notes + ac_circuit_table), captured in the sibling file -- this house's EE set puts these notes on the last EE sheet (EE-06) same as house #2 put them on its own last EE sheet (EE-04).
